tool_args_summary: {"statement": "The host was used to transfer sensitive files to a cloud-sync folder (Dropbox/Google Drive/OneDrive); this will be evident in disk artefacts such as LNK/jumplist entries or prefetch records referencing cloud sync clients or cloud paths.", "specialist": "DISK", "evidence_expected": ["LNK/jumplist entries referencing Dropbox/Google Drive/OneDrive paths", "prefetch records for DROPBOX.EXE, GOOGLEDRIVEFS.EXE, ONEDRIVE.EXE", "AutomaticDestinations or Recent files showing cloud-sync paths"]}
{"query": "Dropbox OR Google Drive OR OneDrive OR DROPBOX.EXE OR GOOGLEDRIVEFS.EXE OR ONEDRIVE.EXE OR \"OneDrive -\"", "limit": 200}

{"question": "Search the disk evidence index for artefacts indicating cloud-sync usage: terms dropbox OR googledrive OR googledrivefs OR onedrive OR \"Google Drive\"; return hits from lnk, prefetch, jumplist_auto, and Any Recent files that reference cloud paths or client executable names. Cite record ids.", "hypothesis_id": "H-001"}

{"statement": "The user account 'fredr' performed the cloud-sync activity (accessing/uploading files to Dropbox/Google Drive/OneDrive), which will be evident in per-user LNK/jumplist and NTUSER/UsrClass entries tied to fredr.", "specialist": "DISK", "evidence_expected": ["lnk/jumplist entries under fredr referencing cloud paths", "NTUSER/UsrClass entries (UserAssist, RecentDocs) showing access by fredr"]}

{"question": "Return the NTUSER/UsrClass and per-user LNK/jumplist evidence tied to the account fredr that show cloud-sync access (provide record ids and exact quoted spans for citation). Focus on LNK records under Users/fredr and AutomaticDestinations for fredr.", "hypothesis_id": "H-002"}

{"query": "Exported-PST OR \"SRL-EMAIL-EXPORT\" OR \"Files of interest\" OR \"Research to Weaponize the Ion Thruster\" OR \"RareEarthDeposits_Confidential\"", "source_tool": "lnk", "limit": 100}

{"question": "Search lnk and jumplist (AutomaticDestinations) artifacts for Recent shortcuts or jumplist targets referencing these filenames/strings under Users/fredr: \"Exported-PST\", \"SRL-EMAIL-EXPORT\", \"Files of interest\", \"Research to Weaponize the Ion Thruster\", \"RareEarthDeposits_Confidential\", \"Exported-PST.lnk\", \"SRL-EMAIL-EXPORT.lnk\". Return matching record ids and verbatim spans for citation.", "hypothesis_id": "H-003"}

{"text": "Recent LNK record shows: \"LNK target=C:\\Users\\fredr\\Your team Dropbox\\Fred Rocba\\Camera Uploads\\2020-06-24 22.00.13.png workdir=C:\\Users\\fredr\\Your team Dropbox\\Fred Rocba\\Camera Uploads\"", "cited_spans": [{"record_id": 2507915, "span_text": "LNK target=C:\\Users\\fredr\\Your team Dropbox\\Fred Rocba\\Camera Uploads\\2020-06-24 22.00.13.png workdir=C:\\Users\\fredr\\Your team Dropbox\\Fred Rocba\\Camera Uploads"}]}

{"observation_id": "O-045", "text": "The LNK record shows that the user 'fredr' had a Recent shortcut pointing to a file in 'Your team Dropbox\\Fred Rocba\\Camera Uploads', indicating the Dropbox folder was present in the user's profile and the file was referenced via Explorer or another process; this is strong evidence the user accessed the file or it was present in the user's Recent items.", "confidence": "HIGH", "justification": "The cited LNK record (record_id 2507915) contains the exact target and workdir path inside the user's Dropbox folder, confirming the shortcut references that file path. LNK Recent entries are created when files are opened or accessed via Explorer, and thus indicate user interaction.", "what_would_change_this_confidence": "If the LNK file timestamp or MFT/USN entries showed it was created by a system process or another account, confidence would decrease. Corroboration with process execution logs (prefetch, amcache, Event logs) would further increase confidence."}

tool_args_summary: {"statement": "User 'fredr' exported a PST named 'Exported-PST' and transferred/synced it to a cloud sync client (Google Drive, Dropbox, or OneDrive) from this host; confirming this will require LNK/jumplist entries or sync-client artifacts in fredr's profile referencing Exported-PST or cloud storage paths.", "specialist": "DISK", "evidence_expected": ["lnk/Users/fredr/.../Exported-PST.lnk", "prefetch/GoogleDrive/Dropbox/OneDrive entries", "jumplist entries referencing cloud paths"]}

{"from_hypothesis_id": "H-004", "to_statement": "A Recent shortcut in user 'fredr' profile references G:\\My Drive\\STARK-RESEARCH-LABS FOLDER\\Exported-PST and an SRL-EMAIL-EXPORT.pst inside it, indicating the Exported-PST folder and SRL-EMAIL-EXPORT.pst were present or referenced by a process that generates Explorer shortcuts (does not by itself prove user-initiated export/transfer).", "reason": "LNK records 2507943 and 2508004 show Recent shortcuts referencing G:\\My Drive\\STARK-RESEARCH-LABS FOLDER\\Exported-PST and SRL-EMAIL-EXPORT.pst, which supports presence but not proof of user action.", "evidence_expected": null}

{"text": "Evidence shows the LNK content: \"LNK target=G:\\My Drive\\STARK-RESEARCH-LABS FOLDER\\Exported-PST\".", "cited_spans": [{"record_id": 2507943, "span_text": "LNK target=G:\\My Drive\\STARK-RESEARCH-LABS FOLDER\\Exported-PST"}]}

{"observation_id": "O-046", "text": "The LNK record indicates a Recent shortcut whose target is 'G:\\My Drive\\STARK-RESEARCH-LABS FOLDER\\Exported-PST', which suggests the Exported-PST folder existed accessible via a mapped G: drive to 'My Drive' (Google Drive/Drive File Stream). This is indicative of a cloud-sync path (Google Drive 'My Drive') being referenced by Explorer or a sync client. Confidence: HIGH.", "confidence": "HIGH", "justification": "The LNK target string explicitly contains 'G:\\My Drive' and 'STARK-RESEARCH-LABS FOLDER\\Exported-PST', which are consistent with Google Drive File Stream mapped drives and Recent shortcut semantics. The LNK was found in fredr's Recent folder, making the user profile context clear.", "what_would_change_this_confidence": "Finding other artifacts linking G:\\My Drive to a specific sync client (prefetch for GoogleDriveFS, registry keys, SRUM records) would raise confidence; absence of such artifacts or evidence that G: was a different mount would lower confidence."

{"text": "Evidence shows the LNK content: \"LNK target=G:\\My Drive\\STARK-RESEARCH-LABS FOLDER\\Exported-PST\\SRL-EMAIL-EXPORT.pst workdir=G:\\My Drive\\STARK-RESEARCH-LABS FOLDER\\Exported-PST\".", "cited_spans": [{"record_id": 2508004, "span_text": "LNK target=G:\\My Drive\\STARK-RESEARCH-LABS FOLDER\\Exported-PST\\SRL-EMAIL-EXPORT.pst workdir=G:\\My Drive\\STARK-RESEARCH-LABS FOLDER\\Exported-PST"}]}

{"observation_id": "O-047", "text": "The LNK record in fredr's Recent folder references a PST file path on G:\\My Drive\\STARK-RESEARCH-LABS FOLDER\\Exported-PST and sets workdir accordingly; this indicates the PST file SRL-EMAIL-EXPORT.pst was present at that path or referenced by a process that writes Explorer Recent shortcuts. It does not by itself prove the user opened the PST or manually uploaded it to cloud storage. Confidence: MEDIUM.", "confidence": "MEDIUM", "justification": "The LNK target and workdir explicitly mention the PST path and the path is on G:\\My Drive, which is consistent with Google Drive File Stream; however LNK entries can be created by sync clients or automated processes, so user action is not directly proven by these records alone.", "what_would_change_this_confidence": "Finding timestamps for the .lnk file and the target PST file, Jump List entries showing application launch, prefetch entries for the application that opened the PST (e.g., OUTLOOK.EXE prefetch with recent timestamp

{"statement": "Google Drive File Stream (DriveFS) and Dropbox clients were installed and executed on the host, and the presence of LNK entries referencing G:\\My Drive together with prefetch records for GOOGLEDRIVEFS.EXE and DROPBOX.EXE indicate cloud sync clients ran (this suggests but does not alone prove that G: was a DriveFS mapping).", "specialist": "DISK", "evidence_expected": ["prefetch/GOOGLEDRIVEFS.EXE entries", "prefetch/DROPBOX.EXE entries", "lnk entries referencing G:\\My Drive"]}
{"text": "Prefetch record shows: \"Prefetch exe=GOOGLEDRIVEFS.EXE run_count=22\".", "cited_spans": [{"record_id": 2505148, "span_text": "Prefetch exe=GOOGLEDRIVEFS.EXE run_count=22"}]}

{"observation_id": "O-048", "text": "The prefetch entry for GOOGLEDRIVEFS.EXE with run_count=22 indicates Google Drive File Stream (DriveFS) executed on the host multiple times, which supports that the G: 'My Drive' path is likely a DriveFS mapping. Confidence: HIGH.", "confidence": "HIGH", "justification": "GOOGLEDRIVEFS.EXE appears in multiple prefetch records with substantial run counts and references to Program Files\\Google\\Drive File Stream in the file list, indicating DriveFS was installed and executed.", "what_would_change_this_confidence": "Finding registry MountPoints2 entries mapping G: to DriveFS, DriveFS log files, or DriveFS process listings in memory would further corroborate the mapping; absence of mapping artifacts would lower confidence."}

{"text": "Prefetch record shows Dropbox prefetch: \"Prefetch exe=DROPBOX.EXE run_count=2\" and DROPBOXUPDATE.EXE run_count=458.", "cited_spans": [{"record_id": 2505136, "span_text": "Prefetch exe=DROPBOX.EXE run_count=2"}, {"record_id": 2505138, "span_text": "Prefetch exe=DROPBOXUPDATE.EXE run_count=458"}]}

{"observation_id": "O-049", "text": "The prefetch entries for DROPBOX.EXE, DROPBOXUPDATE.EXE and ShimCache/Amcache entries referencing Dropbox indicate Dropbox was installed and executed on the host; SRUM network usage shows significant bytes sent/received by Dropbox, consistent with active syncing behavior. Confidence: HIGH.", "confidence": "HIGH", "justification": "Multiple prefetch records reference Dropbox executables, Amcache entry shows Dropbox.exe, and SRUM app network_usage records list program files path for Dropbox with non-zero bytes sent/received.", "what_would_change_this_confidence": "Finding Dropbox config/log files, MountPoints/shortcut entries pointing to Dropbox paths, or Dropbox process evidence in memory would further increase confidence; absence of additional Dropbox artifacts would lower confidence."}

history.statement: User 'fredr' exported a PST named 'Exported-PST' and transferred/synced it to a cloud sync client (Google Drive, Dropbox, or OneDrive) from this host; confirming this will require LNK/jumplist entries or sync-client artifacts in fredr's profile referencing Exported-PST or cloud storage paths.

A Recent shortcut in user 'fredr' profile references G:\My Drive\STARK-RESEARCH-LABS FOLDER\Exported-PST and an SRL-EMAIL-EXPORT.pst inside it, indicating the Exported-PST folder and SRL-EMAIL-EXPORT.pst were present or referenced by a process that generates Explorer shortcuts (does not by itself prove user-initiated export/transfer).

Google Drive File Stream (DriveFS) and Dropbox clients were installed and executed on the host, and the presence of LNK entries referencing G:\My Drive together with prefetch records for GOOGLEDRIVEFS.EXE and DROPBOX.EXE indicate cloud sync clients ran (this suggests but does not alone prove that G: was a DriveFS mapping).

tool_args_summary: {"statement": "If this host was compromised, Sigma detections will flag suspicious authentication or execution (e.g., RDP logons, PowerShell, service creation) around the intrusion window in the parsed event logs.", "specialist": "LOG", "evidence_expected": ["High/critical Sigma detections referencing Security.evtx or PowerShell Operational/Admin logs", "Event IDs consistent with logon (4624/4625), RDP (LogonType 10), or suspicious process creation (4688)"]}
{"query": "rule=\"Remote Desktop\" OR LogonType=10 OR mstsc OR RDP", "source_tool": "sigma:medium", "limit": 50}
{"query": "LogonType 10 OR LogonType=10 OR mstsc OR RDP", "source_tool": "sigma:medium", "limit": 50}

{"question": "Corroborate cloud-sync mechanisms and potential exfil paths: (1) confirm Google Drive File Stream (DriveFS) artifacts mapping to a drive letter (e.g., G:\\My Drive) via prefetch content and registry MountedDevices/MountPoints2; (2) corroborate Dropbox usage beyond prefetch by locating Amcache/ShimCache entries and SRUM network usage for Dropbox/GoogleDriveFS/OneDrive.", "hypothesis_id": "H-007"}
